root_text.an7.39:1.1: “Sattahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato sāriputto catasso paṭisambhidā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an7.39:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, sāriputto ‘idaṁ me cetaso līnattan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti;
root_text.an7.39:1.4: ajjhattaṁ saṅkhittaṁ vā cittaṁ ‘ajjhattaṁ me saṅkhittaṁ cittan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti;
root_text.an7.39:1.5: bahiddhā vikkhittaṁ vā cittaṁ ‘bahiddhā me vikkhittaṁ cittan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti;
root_text.an7.39:1.6: tassa viditā vedanā uppajjanti, viditā upaṭṭhahanti, viditā abbhatthaṁ gacchanti;
root_text.an7.39:1.8: vitakkā uppajjanti, viditā upaṭṭhahanti, viditā abbhatthaṁ gacchanti;
root_text.an7.39:1.9: sappāyāsappāyesu kho panassa dhammesu hīnappaṇītesu kaṇhasukkasappaṭibhāgesu nimittaṁ suggahitaṁ sumanasikataṁ sūpadhāritaṁ suppaṭividdhaṁ paññāya.
root_text.an7.39:1.10: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, sattahi dhammehi samannāgato sāriputto catasso paṭisambhidā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharatī”ti.

translation_text.an7.39:1.1: “Mendicants, having seven qualities, Sāriputta realized the four kinds of textual analysis and lives having achieved them with his own insight.
translation_text.an7.39:1.3: It’s when Sāriputta truly understood: ‘This is mental sluggishness’.
translation_text.an7.39:1.4: He truly understood internally constricted mind as ‘internally constricted mind’.
translation_text.an7.39:1.5: He truly understood externally scattered mind as ‘externally scattered mind’.
translation_text.an7.39:1.6: He knew feelings, perceptions, and thoughts as they arose, as they remained, and as they went away.
translation_text.an7.39:1.9: The patterns of qualities—suitable or unsuitable, inferior or superior, or those on the side of dark or bright—were properly grasped, focused on, borne in mind, and penetrated with wisdom.
translation_text.an7.39:1.10: Having these seven qualities, Sāriputta realized the four kinds of textual analysis and lives having achieved them with his own insight.”
